back_translation: Now a certain man named Ananias and Sapphira his wife sold their piece of land.
And he embezzled some of the money as his own, his wife also being aware of it, and brought only a portion and laid it at the feet of the apostles.
Then Peter said, 'Ananias, why has Satan taken hold of your heart, so that you have lied to the Holy Spirit and embezzled some of the money from that land?

When it had not yet been sold, was not that land yours? And when it was sold, was not the money also under your authority? Why then did you think to do such a thing in your heart? You have not lied to men, but to God.'
When Ananias heard these words, he fell down and died. And great fear arose over everyone who heard of the event.
Then the young men rose up, wrapped his body, and carried him out to bury him.
After about three hours, his wife entered, not knowing what had happened.
Peter asked her, 'Tell us, did you two sell that piece of land for this price?' She answered, 'Yes, for this price indeed.'
Peter then said to her, 'Why did you two conspire together to test the Spirit of the Lord? Behold, the feet of those who buried your husband are at the door, and they will carry you out also.'
At that instant she fell down at Peter's feet and died. The young men came in and found her dead, so they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
And great fear arose over the whole church, and over everyone who heard of these events.
The apostles performed many signs and wonders among the people, and all the believers were gathered together with one accord in Solomon's Portico.
As for the rest, no one dared come and join them, but the people held them in high esteem.
And moreover, believers — both men and women in great numbers — were being added to the Lord all the more.
So much so that people brought the sick out into the streets and laid them on small beds and mats, so that at least the shadow of Peter might fall on some one of them when he walked by.
And crowds from the towns surrounding Jerusalem also came, bringing the sick and those harassed by evil spirits, and all of them received healing.
Then the high priest and all those on his side, who were the Sadducees, were filled with jealousy, and rose up.
And they laid hands on the apostles, taking them to be detained in the public jail.
But during the night, an angel of the Lord opened the doors of the prison and brought the apostles out, and said,
'Go, stand in the temple courtyard and proclaim all the words of this life to the people.'

When they heard this, they entered the temple courtyard at daybreak and began to teach. Now the high priest and those on his side arrived and convened the Jewish council, that is, all the elders of the nation of Israel, and sent someone to the jail to bring the apostles.
But the officers who went there did not find them in the prison, so they returned to report,
saying, 'We found the prison locked tightly on every side, and the guards standing at the doors; but when we opened the doors and went inside, we found no one at all.'
When the captain of the temple guard and the chief priests heard this report, they were utterly perplexed and did not understand how this whole matter would end.
At that moment someone came in and reported to them, 'Behold, the men whom you arrested and imprisoned are now standing and teaching the people in the temple courtyard!'
Then the captain of the temple guard went with the officers and brought the apostles — but not by force, because they feared that the people would stone them.
When they had brought the apostles in, they made them stand before the Jewish council, and the high priest interrogated them,
saying, 'We have strictly commanded you not to teach in this name, yet behold, you have made your teaching fill Jerusalem, and you are determined to bring the blood of this man upon us.'
Peter and the apostles replied, 'We must obey God rather than obey men.
The God of our fathers has raised Jesus to life — the one whom you killed by hanging him on a tree.
God has exalted this Jesus to his right hand, making him Prince and Savior, to grant repentance and the forgiveness of sins to Israel.
We are witnesses of these events, and the Holy Spirit, whom God has given to those who obey him, is also a witness.'
When the members of the Jewish council heard this, they were enraged, and resolved to kill the apostles.
But there was a certain man in the council, a Pharisee named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law honored by all the people, who rose up and commanded that the apostles be taken outside for a short while.
Then he said to them, 'Men of Israel, be very careful about what you are about to do to these men.

For some time ago, there was Theudas, who rose up claiming to be a great one, and about four hundred men went to join him. But he was executed, and all those who followed him were scattered, ending without any success.
After that one, there was Judas the Galilean, who rose up in the days of the census, and persuaded people to follow him. He too collapsed, and all those who followed him were scattered.
Therefore in such a case, I advise that you leave these men alone — do not bother them. For if this plan or this enterprise arose from men, it will collapse of itself.
But if it arose from God, you will not be able to destroy them. Beware lest you be found to be those who fight against God.' And they were persuaded by his words.
Then they called the apostles in, had them flogged, and commanded them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and then released them.
Then the apostles went out from the Jewish council rejoicing, because God had counted them worthy to suffer disgrace for that Name.
And every day, both in the temple courtyard and from house to house, they did not cease teaching and proclaiming the good news that Jesus is the Christ.
